prevalence.trend: stable to slowly decreasing with infant vaccination scale-up; higher prevalence in some tribal and high-risk groups

incidence.value: variable; ongoing adult transmission in risk networks

riskFactors: perinatal transmission without timely birth dose
vulnerablePopulations: newborns without timely birth dose vaccination
dialysis patients and people receiving frequent transfusions
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: HBsAg for diagnosis; HBV DNA and ALT for treatment decisions; fibrosis staging as available; per national/WHO guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: antenatal screening variable across states; screening more common in blood banks and high-risk settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to diagnosis and linkage gaps; treatment more available in urban centers

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: HBsAg screening in blood donation and antenatal care (heterogeneous); viral load testing concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Tenofovir/entecavir for eligible chronic HBV; infant vaccination with birth dose and pentavalent schedule; PMTCT where implemented
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Uneven birth dose coverage and limited access to HBV DNA testing outside cities, leading to delayed staging and treatment
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Large-scale immunization programme and availability of affordable generic antivirals
